channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Daviplata refuerza su operación en Colombia y El Salvador, y podría retomar su expansión internacional en 2027
item.description: En pleno auge de las billeteras digitales en la región, DaviPlata apuesta por consolidar su operación en Colombia y potenciar su presencia en El Salvador antes de dar el salto a nuevos mercados. La expansión internacional, que no está prevista para este año, podría retomarse en 2027 como parte de su nueva estrategia. “Primero, este año será muy intenso para nuestra operación en Colombia.

item.title: Insurers face complex, fast-moving risk landscape as fragile ceasefire throws Strait of Hormuz into turmoil
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘Bigger than an industrial revolution’: Australian MGA Kaibridge eyes growth with AI-native platform
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: How to Put People First in Your AI Rollout
item.description: Most importantly, it requires investment in your most valuable resource: your people. "We made a deliberate decision to treat employee confidence as the primary KPI of our AI transformation." When it comes to AI in the insurance industry, 2026 is shaping up to be a defining year. Adoption is accelerating, investment is increasing, and nearly two-thirds of independent agents say they're optimistic that AI can support their work.

item.title: AI as a Tool or AI as a Product?
item.description: Both mistakes come from not recognizing when a task requires a product. The gap between $20 ChatGPT and six-figure AI vendors lies in integration, repeatability and operational complexity personal tools can't address. Someone on your team just demoed how ChatGPT or Copilot can extract data from a medical report in seconds.

item.title: Empathy LifeVault now available in Canada
item.description: Empathy has launched its digital legacy planning platform, LifeVault, in Canada through a partnership with Serenia Life Financial . policyholders through partnerships with leading financial institutions and insurers, and we’re proud to now offer LifeVault to families across Canada. LifeVault enables users to create and manage estate documents such as wills, powers of attorney, and advance care directives, while providing secure storage and sharing of financial information.

item.title: Building confidence in your agency’s tech stack
item.description: How to evaluate your agency’s tech stack for flexibility and growth Agencies looking to close the readiness gap should ask themselves, “What will help us operate with more confidence and flexibility?” An evaluation doesn’t require a full overhaul. A flexible tech stack makes it easier to reduce manual work, improve service, and add new capabilities without disrupting the workflows your team depends on. Many agencies have a tech stack that serves their business.

item.title: Cytora Integrates VulnCheck Data to Enhance Cyber Underwriting
item.description: Cytora (London) has integrated exploit intelligence from VulnCheck (Lexington, Mass.), in collaboration with Infinite Insight (London), into its risk processing platform to support more granular cyber underwriting. By integrating VulnCheck’s intelligence into Cytora’s platform, insurers get the deepest context on threat activity, enabling more accurate risk differentiation and underwriting confidence .” Cytora says the integration is part of its broader effort to expand data-driven underwriting capabilities across commercial and specialty lines. This enables our clients to move beyond basic questionnaires and access actionable intelligence that drives smarter, faster, and more resilient cyber underwriting decisions .” According to the company, the capability allows underwriters to assess a prospective insured’s digital footprint and evaluate exposure based on active exploit data rather than static submission information.

item.title: Chubb Names Kevin Rampe Global Head of Claims
item.description: Chubb Limited today announced that Kevin Rampe has been named senior vice president, Chubb Group, global claims officer, responsible for global claims management. Rampe will also retain his responsibilities as head of North America claims. In his expanded position, Rampe will lead the company’s claims organization globally and be responsible for all aspects of executive claims management, service and administration for Chubb through its worldwide network of claims offices.

item.title: Wildfires Race Across US as Drought Spans Half the Nation
item.description: Heat combined with a dry La Niña winter has helped drive a sprawling drought across the Southeast and central US. It now spans half the country and roughly 90% of the southern US, leaving ample stores of tinder-dry grass and brush to feed any stray spark. An unusually hot, snowless winter has fueled a fast start to the spring fire season across large swaths of the western, central and southeastern US, disrupting businesses and upending daily life.

item.title: Vehicle Customization, Strong Used Car Market Create Headache for Auto Underwriters
item.description: The sheer number of vehicles with unique configurations, along with a volatile used car market, is disrupting accurate insurance valuation and cutting into insurer profits, according to a new report by J.D. Within the large pickup truck segment, the Ford F-150 currently has upwards of 100,000 unique build configurations and, market-wide, more than 600,000 unique vehicle configurations were sold in the United States in the last year alone, according to JD Power data. In addition, used-vehicle retail prices have risen 20% in the past five years.

item.title: What Men and Women Think About Gender and Pay: AP-NORC poll
item.description: Most women who are employed full-time — about 6 in 10 — say men have more opportunities when it comes to earning competitive wages, according to the survey from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research , while about one-third think neither gender has an advantage. Equal pay emerged as a major source of concern for working women in the poll and an area where men and women are far apart in their perception of gender equity. About 3 in 10 employed women say they have personally experienced wage discrimination because of their gender.

item.title: Meet the New AI Coworker Who Won’t Stop Snitching to Your Boss
item.description: The Slack messages began arriving at 5:47 a.m. Three sales proposals had gone out the previous week and none of the team members had scheduled follow-ups. The reminders were crisp, professional and relentless — and they hadn’t been sent by a human.

item.title: Exclusive: Juno, CPA-Founded Startup That Aims To Make Tax Returns Less Painful With AI, Raises $12M
item.description: Addressing both the opportunities — and risks — that came with advances in AI, Haase started building Juno , a tax prep automation startup, on the side in 2023. “We continuously ‘dog fed’ the early Juno prototypes into the firm to see what worked best, what slowed things down, and to make it the most efficient tax preparation platform as possible,” Haase said. As of today, the majority of small to mid-sized accounting firms — which make up 90% of the market — remain stuck in a cycle of manual data entry.

item.title: CSU forecasts “somewhat below-normal” 2026 Atlantic hurricane season
item.description: The Colorado State University (CSU) tropical meteorology team has issued its first forecast for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season , calling for “somewhat below-normal” levels of activity, citing the potential for a robust El Niño and associated increases in vertical wind shear as key factors. Phil Klotzbach, Senior Research Scientist in the Department of Atmospheric Science at Colorado State University and a leader on the team delivering these forecasts, said indications are for a “somewhat below-normal season”, with a “high likelihood of robust El Nino and associated increases in vertical wind shear the primary factor.” The CSU April forecast calls for 13 named tropical storms in the 2026 Atlantic season, with 6 becoming hurricanes and 2 achieving Category 3 strength or greater to become major hurricanes. Klotzbach further stated, “The primary reason for CSU’s somewhat below-normal Atlantic hurricane season forecast is a high likelihood for a robust El Nino.

item.title: Mapfre Re seeks $200m US named storm cover with third Recoletos Re catastrophe bond
item.description: Mapfre Re, the Spanish global reinsurance company, is back in the catastrophe bond market to sponsor its third deal, seeking $200 million or more in US named storm protection from a Recoletos Re DAC (Series 2026-1) issuance, Artemis can report. In December 2024, the company secured $125 million of annual aggregate US named storm retrocession from the capital markets with its debut Recoletos Re DAC (Series 2024-1) catastrophe bond. As with the first two cat bonds, Mapfre Re is again using its Ireland-based issuance vehicle Recoletos Re DAC for its third catastrophe bond sponsorship.

item.title: One Alliance North America lifts target for One Shield Re cat bond to as much as $125m
item.description: One Alliance North America entered the catastrophe bond market for the first time in mid-March, with an initial target to secure $100 million or more in multi-peril reinsurance through its debut One Shield Re Series 2026-1 issuance. One Alliance North America Insurance Company, the US domestic market homeowners and commercial lines specialist, has now raised the target size for its debut One Shield Re Ltd. The cat bond is designed to provide One Alliance North America with a three-year source of named storm and wildfire reinsurance, on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis, running from issuance through to April 2029.

item.title: Anthony Izzo joins AXIS Capital as CCO
item.description: AXIS Capital Holdings Limited, a Bermuda-based global insurer and reinsurer, has announced the appointment of Anthony Izzo as Chief Commercial Officer (CCO), based at the company’s Red Bank and New York offices. Izzo, drawing on 35 years of industry experience, has been tasked with leading the company’s global commercial strategy, working closely with its underwriting and regional leadership to enhance how AXIS engages with brokers and partners. Izzo will be reporting to Vince Tizzio, President and CEO of AXIS, who commented: “Throughout his career, Tony has developed and grown strong commercial partnerships and mutually beneficial strategic relationships while driving disciplined growth across complex specialty markets.

item.title: Daiichi to reinsure block of whole life and annuity policies with Prismic Life
item.description: is set to reinsure a Yen-denominated in-force block of whole life and annuity policies with Bermuda-based life and annuity reinsurer and Prismic subsidiary, Prismic Life Reinsurance. As per the terms, Daiichi’s obligations to policyholders will remain unchanged following the reinsurance agreement, and the firm will continue to administer and service the policies. The reinsurer explained that its business model combines insurance expertise with operational scalability and a long-term investment approach, enabling it to provide customised reinsurance and balance sheet management solutions across life and annuity products.

item.title: Property rate declines to pressure top-line growth in 2026: TD Cowen
item.description: According to recent analysis by US-based investment bank and research firm, TD Cowen, rate declines, most notably in property, are pressuring top-line growth and underwriting results for specialty and reinsurance carriers in 2026. TD Cowen’s analysts said, “These rate declines at April renewals are part of a continued trend in global reinsurance as they follow 10-15% rate decreases at the January renewals at the start of this year. We expect pricing pressure in property-cat reinsurance to persist through 2026, including the FL mid-year renewals.” Looking ahead to the mid-yer renewals, which are focused on the US and notably Florida, analysts said that Florida should see some incremental reinsurance demand given the shift of more than 600,000 policies since year-end 2024 from Citizens to private insurers.

item.title: LexisNexis: Synthetic Identity Fraud Rises Significantly in 2025
item.description: A recent report by LexisNexis Risk Solutions found synthetic identity fraud is on the rise. According to its latest Cybercrime Report based on more than 100 billion online transactions in 2025, LexisNexis said 11% of frauds now involve a synthetic identity, representing an eight-fold global increase over 2024. Increasingly, attackers rely on advanced bots and AI-driven tools to mimic human behavior and test defenses with unprecedented speed and accuracy,” said Stephen Topliss, vice president of fraud and identity at LexisNexis Risk Solutions.

item.title: Jacksonville Contractor Charged With Underreporting Payroll by $2 Million
item.description: Florida authorities have charged a Jacksonville contractor with underreporting payroll by almost $2 million and stiffing his workers’ compensation insurer for almost $300,000 in premiums. Carlos Antonio Maradiago Garcia, 50, a native of Honduras, was investigated by the Florida Department of Financial Services for insurance fraud allegedly committed in 2021, 2022 and 2023, according to the Jacksonville Sheriff’s arrest report. Florida Secretary of State corporation records show the contractor’s company, Carlos Maradiaga LLC, was incorporated in 2016.

item.title: Maryland Reaches Settlement With Ship Owner, Operator Over Bridge Collapse
item.description: The state of Maryland has reached a settlement in principle with Grace Ocean Private Limited and Synergy Marine Pte Ltd., the owner and operator of the M/V Dali, resolving a portion of the state’s claims arising from the cargo ship’s allision with the Francis Scott Key Bridge two years ago. Judge Allows Dali Manager’s Bid to Limit Liability for Baltimore Bridge Collapse Under the maritime law, a shipowner’s liability for a maritime loss or mishap is limited to the value of the ship and her pending freight if the mishap occurred without the privity of knowledge of the owner. The state of Maryland and wrongful death claimants had sought to prevent Synergy Marine from invoking the Shipowners’ Limitation of Liability Act on the basis that Synergy did not meet the requirements as an owner of the ship and thus is not entitled to limited liability protection granted by the law.

item.title: AXIS Capital strengthens commercial leadership with appointment of Anthony Izzo
item.description: AXIS Capital Holdings has appointed Anthony Izzo as Chief Commercial Officer, in a move aimed at strengthening its global commercial strategy and deepening broker and partner engagement. “His deep and varied experience across distribution, underwriting, and broking will be an asset to our team, as we continue to execute on our specialty leadership strategy and maximize how we bring our products and capabilities to the market.” Izzo joins AXIS Capital from Everest, where he most recently served as EVP and Group Chief Commercial Officer, overseeing enterprise-wide commercial strategy and client relationships. Based between AXIS Capital’s Red Bank and New York offices, Izzo brings 35 years of industry experience to the role.

item.title: Chapter raises $100 million
item.description: Medicare broker Chapter Medicare announced a $100 million Series E round led by Generation Investment Management, with participation from new investors Fifth Down Capital, 8VC, as well as the company’s existing investors: Stripes, XYZ Venture Capital, Addition, Narya Capital, Susa Ventures, and Maverick Ventures. Founded in 2020, the NY-based startup has raised ~$284 million to date. In 2025, the company grew revenue 3x and surpassed $100 million in ARR.

item.title: Daiichi Life to reinsure whole life block with Prismic Life
item.description: Prismic Life Holding Company has entered into an agreement with Dai-ichi Life to reinsure a Yen-denominated in-force block of whole life and annuity policies. “This agreement reflects Prismic’s continued commitment to supporting the Japan insurance market with tailored reinsurance solutions that help insurers efficiently manage their growth, risk, and capital objectives. We value our strong relationship with Daiichi and are pleased to support them with a solution that complements their ongoing servicing and customer commitments.

item.title: XPT Specialty hires Dina Dryden as Vice President in Woodland Hills
item.description: XPT Specialty has appointed Dina Dryden as Vice President – Commercial Underwriter/Broker, based in its Woodland Hills, California office, as the wholesale broker continues to expand its West Coast operations. Prior to that, she held an assistant vice president role at RT Specialty focused on binding authority underwriting for commercial accounts. Dryden brings more than 30 years of experience in commercial property and casualty underwriting, with a focus on excess and surplus lines.

item.title: How Insurance Leaders Can Leverage AI Without Sacrificing Trust
item.description: In 2025 alone, data breaches affected nearly 300 million individuals, underscoring how quickly trust can erode. Advances in AI, especially Gen AI and Agentic AI, are creating significant opportunities but also sharpening a critical question for insurers: just because a process can be automated, should it be? High-impact AI use cases offer the potential to reduce costs and risk; improve productivity and efficiency; and drive revenue across sales, underwriting, claims and servicing.

item.title: Insurer Nationwide Operating Income Up 37% in 2025
item.description: Insurer Nationwide finished 2025 with net operating income up 37% compared with 2024, to $4.3 billion as total sales and premiums were up 7% to a record $73.2 billion. The Columbus, Ohio-based mutual insurance company—entering its 100th year of operation in 2026—released its 2025 operating results late last week. During a year marked by wildfires and severe convective storms, Nationwide said it paid more than $20.2 billion in claims and benefits to policyholders in 2025.

item.title: Australia’s new merger control regime makes ‘positive start’, regulator says
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FSB Regional Consultative Group for Asia meets in Hanoi
item.description: The Financial Stability Board (FSB) Regional Consultative Group for Asia (RCG Asia) convened on 8-9 April 2026 in Hanoi, hosted by the State Bank of Vietnam. Co-chaired by Michele Bullock, Governor of the Reserve Bank of Australia, and Pham Thanh Ha, Deputy Governor of the State Bank of Vietnam, the meeting covered: global and regional financial vulnerabilities, the impact of strains in foreign exchange markets, safety and security of cross-border payments, and vulnerabilities arising from global stablecoin arrangements. Members also reflected on the FSB’s 2026 work programme and discussed how regional perspectives can contribute to global financial stability efforts.

item.title: Global Investors Help Boost Latin America’s Late-Stage Funding Boom In Q1
item.description: A boom in late-stage and growth funding helped buoy venture funding in Latin America for the first quarter of 2026, Crunchbase data shows. Startups in Latin America raised a combined $1.03 billion across seed- and growth-stage deals in the three-month period ending March 31. Historically, Brazil has been the powerhouse in Latin America for venture capital funding.

item.title: Florida Peninsula raises Palm Re 2026-1 cat bond target again, to $250m
item.description: Now, sources have told us that the target size has been raised further, with Florida Peninsula now seeking $250 million in indemnity based Florida named storm reinsurance from its latest cat bond, while the tranche of notes on offer have been lowered once again. With Florida Peninsula raising its target once again for its latest catastrophe bond, as well as lowering the price of the notes on offer once more, it clearly shows that the insurer is looking to capitalise on catastrophe bond investor appetite to potentially gain more reinsurance support from the capital markets at more attractive pricing. Florida Peninsula Insurance Company has for a second time lifted the targeted amount of reinsurance it is seeking from its latest catastrophe bond, with the Palm Re Ltd.

item.title: PERILS raises Windstorm Goretti insured market loss estimate to €479m
item.description: Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has published its second industry loss estimate for extratropical windstorm Goretti, also known as Elli, that affected southwestern England, northern France and Belgium in early January, placing the insurance market loss at EUR 479 million. PERILS has confirmed that an updated estimate of the insurance market loss from Windstorm Goretti will be published on July 9th, 2026, six months after the event end date. As per PERILS, windstorm Goretti also marked the second-largest European windstorm event loss in the 2025/26 season, behind windstorm Nils, which struck one month later.

item.title: Cat bonds – Maintaining an equilibrium in value, for sponsors & investors: ILS NYC 2026 video
item.description: The next video we are publishing is from a panel session focused on the catastrophe bond market segment, titled “Cat bonds: Maintaining an equilibrium in value, for both sponsors and investors.” This cat bond focused panel discussion was kindly moderated by Philipp Kusche , Chairman HCMA Europe, Global Co-Head ILS, Howden Capital Markets & Advisory. Watch the full video from our Artemis ILS NYC 2026 conference to more about conditions in the catastrophe bond marketplace, from the perspective of both investors and sponsors . This new video features another panel discussion from Artemis ILS NYC 2026, a session focused on the cat bond marketplace , from our tenth catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) conference in the ILS NYC series, held on February 6th 2026 in New York.

item.title: Mapfre Re appoints Fernando Utrilla Agüero as CFO
item.description: The Board of Directors of Mapfre Re, the global reinsurance unit of Mapfre Group, has approved the appointment of Fernando Utrilla Agüero as Chief Financial Officer (CFO), succeeding Daniel Quermia. Agüero’s appointment is part of the succession process following Quermia’s upcoming retirement, after nearly 40 years with Mapfre Re. Agüero joined Mapfre Re in May 1994 and has since held various positions of responsibility.

item.title: Smaller pension scheme buy-ins rise 30% in 2025: Hymans Robertson
item.description: While 2025 saw a more than 20% fall in bulk annuity volumes year-on-year, the volume of buy-in deals less than £100 million in value increased by over 30%, according to Hymans Robertson’s latest report. Hymans Robertson also noted that 2025 saw developments in the alternative risk transfer space, with TPT announcing plans to launch a superfund. Michael Abramson, Partner and Risk Transfer Specialist at Hymans Robertson, said, “2025 saw a more than 20% fall in bulk annuity volumes year-on-year.

item.title: AXA XL appoints Poonam Jivram as Head of Motor Underwriting, UK & Lloyd’s
item.description: AXA XL, the property, casualty, and specialty risk division of AXA, has announced the appointment of Poonam Jivram as Head of Motor Underwriting, UK & Lloyd’s. Based in London, Jivram will be responsible for developing and executing the strategic underwriting direction for the UK & Lloyd’s motor fleet insurance business, driving profitable growth and strengthening relationships with brokers and clients. Ben Kinsella, Head of Risk Managed Underwriting, UK & Lloyd’s at AXA XL, commented, “One of the major challenges facing the commercial motor fleet market is the growth in electric vehicles and the differing risk profile these vehicles pose.

item.title: China Confirms Three Ships Passed Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Three Chinese ships recently sailed through the Strait of Hormuz after coordination with relevant parties, a foreign ministry spokesperson told a regular daily press briefing on Tuesday, while calling for peace and stability in the Gulf Region. Ship-tracking data showed two Chinese container ships sailed through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday on their second attempt to leave the Gulf after turning back on Friday. “The Strait of Hormuz and the surrounding waters are an important route for global trade and energy supplies.

item.title: Trump Appeals Civil Fraud Ruling, Citing Former Lawyer’s Testimony Doubts
item.description: Donald Trump has asked New York’s highest appeals court to vacate a judge’s finding that he was liable for inflating the value of his real estate assets, arguing that his former lawyer Michael Cohen has since cast doubt on his own testimony against the president. In a filing on Wednesday, Trump’s lawyers cited a January Substack post by Cohen in which he claimed he felt “compelled and coerced” to deliver testimony that New York Attorney General Letitia James was seeking in a civil fraud suit she brought against Trump. In March, Trump’s legal team asked the same appeals court to compel James’ office to produce all its records pertaining to Cohen, arguing his testimony was “at the heart of the NYAG’s case.” In response to a request for comment on Wednesday’s appeal, Cohen said he stood by his Substack post.

item.title: DOJ Top Antitrust Litigators Exit After Ticketmaster Accord
item.description: The Justice Department settled an antitrust case accusing Live Nation of illegally monopolizing the live events industry one week after the start of a New York federal jury trial. The US Justice Department’s top antitrust litigator and three senior trial attorneys that led cases against Live Nation Entertainment Inc., Apple Inc. Some of the departures stem from anger among the antitrust staff about a surprise March 9 settlement with Live Nation and concerns about whether the division is committed to litigating pending cases, according to the people, who asked not to be named discussing internal agency dynamics.

item.title: BOXX Insurance expands distribution as cyber solutions go live on mShift Marketplace
item.description: The post BOXX Insurance expands distribution as cyber solutions go live on mShift Marketplace appeared first on Insurtech Insights . BOXX Insurance has made its cyber insurance solutions available on the Millennial Shift Technologies (mShift) Marketplace, in a move aimed at expanding digital distribution and improving broker access to cyber cover. “By bringing BOXX Insurance onto the mShift Marketplace, we’re giving brokers faster access to innovative cyber insurance and protection solutions while simplifying the placement process through a fully digital experience.” Jim Miller added: “BOXX’s partnership with mShift benefits our broker partners as it expands the accessibility of our all-in-one cyber insurance offerings for SMEs.

item.title: $53m profit: VIG Re posts 17.8% earnings growth on strong underwriting and investment performance
item.description: The post $53m profit: VIG Re posts 17.8% earnings growth on strong underwriting and investment performance appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Strong profitability, a robust solvency position, and a resilient balance sheet enable us to fund growth priorities under VIGRe28, deploy capital selectively, and sustain long-term value creation in a volatile risk environment.” The results underline continued profitability momentum among reinsurers, as disciplined underwriting and lower catastrophe losses support earnings, while investment income remains a key contributor in a higher-rate environment. VIG Re has reported a 17.8% year-on-year increase in profit before tax to €49m (approximately $53m) for FY’25, supported by disciplined underwriting, effective risk management, and solid investment performance.

item.title: Atrium deploys AI-powered ‘BrokerBrief’ with Sofix to accelerate claims handling at Lloyd’s
item.description: The post Atrium deploys AI-powered ‘BrokerBrief’ with Sofix to accelerate claims handling at Lloyd’s appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Atrium and Sofix have deployed an AI-powered claims tool, BrokerBrief, aimed at accelerating claims handling and improving decision-making across all classes of business. The rollout marks a further step in Atrium’s technology strategy to enhance claims handling, while reinforcing Sofix’s focus on bringing AI-driven automation to complex claims workflows across the London market.

item.title: INSURICA raises $25 million
item.description: INSURICA has raised $24.8 million in an exempt securities offering, according to a recent Form D filing. INSURICA, established in 1959, is among the 50 largest insurance brokers in the United States and ranks as the 30th largest privately held independent agency. The full amount of $24,766,101 has already been sold to a single investor, with the offering structured as an equity raise under Rule 506(c).

item.title: Chewy acquires Modern Animal to push deeper into pet healthcare
item.description: Chewy is buying Modern Animal , a tech-enabled veterinary platform with 29 clinics and a membership-driven care model, as it looks to expand beyond commerce and into full-stack pet healthcare. Modern Animal contributes more than $125 million in annualized revenue and expands Chewy’s clinic footprint from 18 to 47 locations nationwide. Modern Animal operates a hybrid model that blends physical clinics with 24/7 virtual care, supported by its own software stack.

item.title: Intact takes majority stake in Cartan Trade
item.description: Cartan Trade is deepening its relationship with Intact Insurance, which is set to become the majority shareholder as part of its European expansion strategy. Effective May 1, Cartan Trade will serve as the underwriting platform for Intact’s trade credit insurance business across Europe, combining its underwriting and technology capabilities with Intact’s balance sheet and AA- rating. The structure gives Intact a dedicated platform for trade credit in Europe, while positioning Cartan Trade to scale through additional capital and distribution.

item.title: Ciberseguro: de la póliza reactiva al modelo de resiliencia operativa
item.description: El mercado actual exige una nueva generación de soluciones que transformen el impacto del ciberriesgo en una variable financiera controlada, sin olvidar que la póliza es solo una parte de la compleja ecuación de resiliencia que las empresas deben resolver hoy mismo. En Stoïk, defendemos un modelo donde la prevención no es un evento puntual, sino un proceso continuo. Esta integración operativa garantiza una transparencia total y elimina los costes ocultos derivados de intermediarios externos en momentos donde cada segundo cuenta.

item.title: Alex Perdel, nuevo Chief Growth & Transformation Officer en Guai Insurtech
item.description: Guai Insurtech ha incorporado a Alex Perdel como Chief Growth & Transformation Officer (CGTO), cargo desde el que asumirá la dirección de la estrategia de crecimiento, tecnología e inteligencia artificial de la filial tecnológica de Grupo Cuidacasa. Perdel supervisará también la evolución del ERP propietario GUAI , integrando nuevas automatizaciones y flujos operativos, y coordinará una arquitectura cloud híbrida sobre AWS y Azure que dará soporte a todas las aplicaciones y servicios de inteligencia artificial. Desde este puesto, liderará la automatización de procesos, el desarrollo de plataformas internas y la evolución del ERP corporativo GUAI, columna vertebral digital de la compañía que conecta procesos, datos y herramientas de todas las áreas de negocio.

item.title: Arch impulsa su negocio ciber con dos promociones internas para Europa e Iberia
item.description: Arch Insurance ha promocionado a José Carlos Jiménez Fernández y a Rafael Ortiz Losada a responsables de Riesgos Cibernéticos, el primero para Europa, y el segundo para Iberia, ambos con base en Madrid. Anteriormente responsable de Riesgo Cibernéticos para Iberia, Jiménez Fernández se incorporó a Arch en 2023 y antes de esto fue Cyber Manager para el Sur de Europa, Oriente Medio, y América Latina en Tokio Marine HCC . Los nombramientos, con efecto inmediato, responden al objetivo de impulsar el crecimiento del negocio cyber en el continente y reforzar la estrategia de suscripción y desarrollo de producto.
